title: Profession
description: A profession config — content artifact loaded from polis/configs/professions/{profession_id}.yaml at app startup. Per P1 substrate-design-locked.md D24 + Phase A.2: file-based YAML config (NOT a DB table); matches polis/configs/industries/ pattern. Loaded via lru_cache; hot-reloadable in V0.5+. Each profession declares starter wealth math (for D24 trajectory model), wages math (for needs_tick weekly wage payment), employer institution template (so spawn proc can match a real institution-as-employer per D12), commerce role hints (for D17 vendor_selection + D15 initial commerce-web edges per CEO plan punted-section #1 default), skills baseline, and cognition routing defaults. V0.1.0 ships with 2 starter professions in polis/configs/professions/ (barista, football-manager); founder content authoring extends to ~6-10 per CEO plan §13.

id.description: Slug. Matches the YAML filename (polis/configs/professions/{id}.yaml). Used by character.professions[].profession_id as FK; also matches institution.wage_rates[profession_id] for per-institution wage premium/discount overrides per Codex #17.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in wizard step 3 + recap output.

description.description: One-sentence wizard-card description. Should not exceed ~120 chars.

starter_grant_coin.description: Per P1 D24: starter Coin grant at spawn. Tuned per D24 formula: starter_grant_coin = round_up(max(lot_one_time_purchase × 1.25, 2 × weekly_runway)) where weekly_runway = lot_weekly_upkeep + weekly_consumption_baseline. Covers one-time lot purchase + ~2 weeks operating runway. Worked examples in P0.5 v2: barista=1500, football-manager=3000.

wages_weekly_coin.description: Per P1 D24 + P0.5 v2 empirical tuning: weekly Coin wage paid by employer institution. DEFAULT for the profession; per-institution overrides live on institution.wage_rates per Codex #17. ONS-calibrated where possible (UK ONS occupation × region median weekly earnings per D23 HYBRID calibration source).

wages_distribution_curve.description: V0.5+: replaces wages_weekly_coin with a distribution (mean, p25, p50, p75 from ONS) for stochastic per-employee variation. V0 ships with wages_weekly_coin only; this field reserved.

employer_institution_template.description: Maps to institution.kind. Per P1 D12: spawn proc uses this template to find a matching institution in the char's district when seeding employer_party_id (per Codex B2: loaded in Python before invoking spawn proc; passed as proc parameter). Examples: cafe, football-club, pub, freelance-marketplace.

commerce_role_hints.description: Per P1 D15 + CEO plan punted-section #1 default: hints used during atomic spawn to seed initial commerce-web edges (1 employer + 1 landlord + top-3 vendors per axis ≈ 12 edges). Founder may tune per character archetype at content-authoring time.

preferred_vendor_categories.description: Categories the profession's chars preferentially affinity-seed at spawn (per AFFINITY-PRIMITIVE + D17 score formula).

required_utilities.description: Per D17 utility-goods carve-out: non-need-meeting goods this profession requires (e.g., transit_weekly for non-resident districts).

skills_baseline.description: Per character.schema professions[].skills initial values at spawn. Hand-tuned per profession.

cognition_routing.description: Defaults for character.cognition fields. Per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md §3.4 cadence + DECISION-CHARACTERLIFEWORKFLOW event subscriptions.

version.description: Profession config version. Bump when wage tables/consumption rates change post-alpha-feedback.
